sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.97 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (M.\,T rn uceanu). Is it true that for every positive rational number$r$there exists a finite group$G$such that${| (G)|}/{|G|}=r$? A similar question is answered in the positive for graphs, monoids, partial groups, and posets (R.\,Molinier, Preprint, 2025, ). It is also known that the set$\{| (G)| / |G| G \}$is dense in$[0, + )$(M.\,T rn uceanu, Elemente Math. (2025), ). { R.\,Molinier}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
